description_long: This hotel features a two-story structure with a prominent wooden staircase leading to an upper balcony that spans the length of the building. The exterior walls are finished in a combination of white plaster and dark timber framing, evoking a traditional European or Tudor style. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with reddish-brown tiles, punctuated by three dormer windows with circular panes. The upper floor boasts five symmetrically arranged windows with decorative wooden awnings, while the lower floor has four simpler windows. The building is elevated on wooden supports, creating an open space beneath the main entrance platform. The overall design suggests a rustic, historic charm suitable for a small urban or village hotel.
description: A two-story, timber-framed hotel with a red-tiled roof, upper balcony, and prominent wooden staircase, featuring multiple windows and a rustic European architectural style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main staircase, upper balcony, and five upper windows with decorative awnings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the building, the pitched roof, and the symmetry of the staircases on both sides.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the balcony's length, four lower windows, and the continuation of the timber framing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the end of the balcony, the roof's gable, and a large window with a circular design.
